title: Sidebar views — port & build plan
description: Build order and per-view contracts for each shell sidebar destination. Nav keys match BombaProMaxWPF/ViewModels/ShellViewModel.cs BuildItems(). Source pages live under BombaProMax/Views/* (the MAUI app). Target views land under BombaProMaxWPF/Views/<area>/.

shell.navKeys: dashboard | ventes | achats | caisse | clients | infrastructure | ressources | rapports | parametres
shell.viewBase: UserControl with Background=Transparent, root ScrollViewer when content can overflow
shell.tabbedLayoutPattern: Top-level UserControl hosting a TabControl with the sub-section names below; each tab content is its own UserControl in the same folder
shell.stickyHeaderButton: Démarrer la journée — already wired via ShellViewModel.StartDayCommand; NOT a routed view

styling.accent: NeuAccentBrush (#3FA89F light / #FF8A3D dark) and NeuAccentGradientBrush for hero CTAs

conventions.localization: All user-facing strings go through LanguageManager.Instance["Key"]; add new resx keys before binding.

items.title: Tableau de bord
items.scope: Forecourt overview demo — KPI ring tanks, market pricing, active pumps, spatial hero. Already shipped.

items.title: Ventes

subTabs.title: Lubrifiants & Articles
subTabs.viewModel: VenteLubrifiantsEtArticlesViewModel (already exists in WPF project — reuse)

items.scope: Three sub-tabs hosting a list + detail/popup. Each tab is its own UserControl.
openQuestions: Sub-tabs as TabControl, NavigationView pivot, or segmented pill at top of page?

items.title: Achats
items.scope: Single page: list + create/edit/details/allocation popups. Popups become Wpf.Ui ContentDialog or windowed dialogs.

items.title: Clients
items.scope: Single page: searchable client list + edit drawer/popup.

items.title: Infrastructure
items.scope: Five-tab page consolidating physical-asset configuration. Each tab is a separate UserControl.
openQuestions: 5 tabs is dense — collapse Jaugeage under Réservoirs?

items.title: Ressources
items.scope: Two tabs. First tab is itself a split view of two related lists.

items.title: Rapports
items.scope: Single page: report selector + filters + preview/export.

items.title: Paramètres

subTabs.title: Retrait de stock
subTabs.lockReason: Super admin only — gated by App.CurrentUser.IsSuperAdmin

items.scope: Three tabs. Last tab visibility/enabled bound to IsSuperAdmin.

perViewWorkflow: 1. Author docs/plans/issue-NN-<navKey>-view.md describing layout, tabs, popups → wait for GO.
2. Add LanguageManager keys (fr + ar) for tab titles, column headers, button labels.
3. Create UserControl shell at targetView (Background=Transparent, ScrollViewer if needed, neumorphic chassis).
4. For multi-tab views: TabControl skinned with NeuTabItemStyle (to be authored once, reused).
5. Port the ViewModel(s) from BombaProMax/ViewModels/* — keep service interfaces, swap MAUI Shell.Current.GoToAsync calls for in-shell navigation or popups.
6. Wire in MainWindow.NavItem_Checked → instantiate the UserControl and host it in ContentFrame.
7. dotnet build green; user runtime-validates; append entry to shell-polish-log.json.
openQuestionsGlobal: Sub-tab UI: WPF TabControl, segmented pill bar at top, or a left mini-rail inside each multi-section view? Recommendation: segmented pill bar — matches the pill aesthetic already used in toolbar.
Popups: Wpf.Ui ContentDialog inline, or floating FluentWindow children? Recommendation: ContentDialog for simple forms, FluentWindow for heavy editors with multiple steps.
Routing: instantiate views per-navigation (cheap, current pattern) vs. cache (faster nav, holds state). Recommendation: cache after first build.
Authoring NeuTabItemStyle — required before Ventes (id 2) since it's the first multi-tab view.
